message.content:
OK bro we have list of small tasks you need to fix them all use this session as orchestrator, and spawn sub-session for each task.
Do it 1 by 1. TAsk by task.
use /fix-eslint-workflow skill for it... you need to run eslint checks only for the file described in the each task and fix warnings.
1 task -> then check elsint warnings in the file from the task -> fix them -> fix calls to the fixed functions in other files if needed -> commit changes with proper message -> push to GH -> create PR with proper description. Then move to the next task. Do it for all tasks in the list. Here is the list of tasks:
  https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-678                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-679                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-680
  https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-681 
  https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-682                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-683                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-684                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-685

content.content: Launching skill: fix-eslint-workflow

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/.claude/skills/fix-eslint-workflow

# Fix ESLint Workflow

End-to-end workflow: Jira ticket → branch → eslint audit → fix violations → fix dependents → fix tests → verify.

## Workflow

### 1. Check Jira ticket

Fetch the ticket to understand which files and ESLint rules are in scope:

```bash
grep ATLASSIAN_CLOUD_ID .env | cut -d'=' -f2
```

Use `getJiraIssue` with the cloud ID and ticket key. Identify:

- Which files need fixing
- Which ESLint rule is being enforced

### 2. Create branch from current branch

```bash
git checkout -b RECO-XXX-descriptive-name
```

Branch from current branch (NOT main). Branch name: `RECO-XXX-kebab-case-summary`.

### 3. Run ESLint on target files

```bash
npm run lint -- --max-warnings=0 path/to/file.ts
```

Capture the full error/warning list before making any changes.

### 4. Fix ESLint violations

Plan your fixes before coding:

1. Identify the ESLint violation(s) in the specified file(s)
2. Use Grep to find ALL callers and imports of affected functions
3. Apply the fix using named object params pattern (not shortcuts)
4. Update ALL test files — mock expectations AND assertions
5. Run `npm run test:coverage` and `npm run code-quality-check` — do not stop until both pass
6. Show a summary of all files changed

Apply fixes in this priority order:

#### a. Auto-fix first

```bash
npm run lint:fix
```

#### b. Fix remaining manually

See [references/eslint-patterns.md](references/eslint-patterns.md) for common patterns specific to this repo.

**Most common manual fixes:**

**Named object params (most common RECO fix)**

```typescript
// ❌ Positional params
function myFn(id: string, name: string): void {}

// ✅ Object with named params
function myFn({ id, name }: { id: string; name: string }): void {}
```

**Path aliases (no relative imports)**

```typescript
// ❌
import { Foo } from '../../modules/foo/foo.service';
// ✅
import { Foo } from '@modules/foo/foo.service';
```

**Explicit return types**

```typescript
// ❌
async function getData() {
	return data;
}
// ✅
async function getData(): Promise<Data> {
	return data;
}
```

### 5. Fix ALL callers of changed functions (CRITICAL)

**This step is mandatory.** After changing any function/method signature, you MUST find and update every call site across the entire project — not just the file you edited.

Find all callers:

```bash
grep -r "functionName" src/ test/ --include="*.ts" -l
```

Or use the Grep tool to search for the function name.

Update **every** call site to use the new object param syntax:

```typescript
// ❌ Old call
validate(configRecord);
service.myFn(id, name);

// ✅ New named call
validate({ envConfig: configRecord });
service.myFn({ id, name });
```

**WARNING: Do NOT use rest-spread `{ ...param }` as a shortcut.** It avoids updating callers but changes the semantic (creates a copy) and produces confusing code. Always use explicit named params: `{ envConfig }`, `{ styleId, colorId }`, etc.

Check these locations in order:

1. Same module's controller/service
2. Other modules importing this service
3. CLI commands in `src/modules/cli/`
4. Scripts in `src/scripts/`
5. Test files (`src/unit-tests/`, `test/`)
6. RFC/documentation files (revert accidental changes to unrelated `validate` from `class-validator`)

### 6. Fix tests

Unit tests: `src/unit-tests/*.unit-spec.ts`
E2E tests: `test/*.e2e-spec.ts`

Update mock calls AND assertions to match new signatures:

```typescript
// ❌
service.myFn(id, name);
expect(service.myFn).toHaveBeenCalledWith(id, name);

// ✅
service.myFn({ id, name });
expect(service.myFn).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ id: id, name: name });
```

Run full coverage to confirm — do not stop until all checks pass:

```bash
npm run test:coverage
```

### 7. Verify ESLint on all changed files

```bash
npm run lint
npm run type-check
```

All checks must pass before done.

## Common Mistakes

| Mistake                                       | Fix                                           |
| --------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| Forgetting to update call sites               | Grep for all usages after changing signatures |
| Missing test updates                          | Run `npm run test:unit` after fixing code     |
| Using relative imports                        | Use `@modules/`, `@utils/`, `@dto/` etc.      |
| Breaking return types                         | Add explicit `: Promise<T>` or `: void`       |
| Branching from main instead of current branch | `git checkout -b` from current branch         |

## Resources

- **[references/eslint-patterns.md](references/eslint-patterns.md)**: Repo-specific ESLint rule patterns and fix examples

input.prompt:
You are working on branch `RECO-678-fix-require-object-params-style-copurchases` in `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service`.

The changes for RECO-678 are already made (require-object-params fixes for style-copurchases and frequently-bought-together modules). But there are ESLint warnings remaining that need to be fixed.

## Step 1: Check current ESLint warnings on the target files

```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 \
  src/modules/db/style-copurchases/style-copurchases.service.ts \
  src/modules/db/style-copurchases/models/style-copurchase.model.ts \
  src/modules/cli/commands/populate-style-copurchases.command.ts \
  src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.service.ts \
  src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.controller.ts \
  src/modules/rest/email-recommendations/email-recommendations.service.ts \
  src/unit-tests/populate-style-copurchases.command.unit-spec.ts \
  test/frequently-bought-together.e2e-spec.ts \
  test/rest/email-recommendations.service.e2e-spec.ts
```

Known warnings to fix:
- `style-copurchases.service.ts`: `no-unnecessary-condition` at lines 26, 72
- `frequently-bought-together.controller.ts`: `no-unnecessary-condition` at lines 80-81
- `frequently-bought-together.service.ts`: `no-unnecessary-condition` at lines 67-68, 103, 258, 324-325, 471-472 and `no-unnecessary-type-parameters` at line 258

## Step 2: Fix ESLint warnings

Read each file and fix the warnings:

For `no-unnecessary-condition` with `??` and optional chaining:
- Remove the `??` operator or optional chain if it's not needed
- e.g., `foo?.bar ?? baz` → `foo.bar ?? baz` if foo is definitely non-null, or just `foo.bar` if bar is never null

For `no-unnecessary-type-parameters`:
- Remove unused generic type parameters from function signatures

Read the files at the specific line numbers to understand context before fixing.

## Step 3: Run tests

```bash
cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service && npm run test:unit 2>&1 | tail -50
```

If unit tests pass, then run e2e tests:
```bash
npm run test:e2e 2>&1 | tail -80
```

## Step 4: Verify ESLint clean

```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 \
  src/modules/db/style-copurchases/style-copurchases.service.ts \
  src/modules/db/style-copurchases/models/style-copurchase.model.ts \
  src/modules/cli/commands/populate-style-copurchases.command.ts \
  src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.service.ts \
  src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.controller.ts \
  src/modules/rest/email-recommendations/email-recommendations.service.ts
```

## Step 5: Commit all changes

Stage all modified files and commit:
```bash
git add src/modules/cli/commands/populate-style-copurchases.command.ts \
  src/modules/db/style-copurchases/models/style-copurchase.model.ts \
  src/modules/db/style-copurchases/style-copurchases.service.ts \
  src/modules/rest/email-recommendations/email-recommendations.service.ts \
  src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.controller.ts \
  src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.service.ts \
  src/unit-tests/populate-style-copurchases.command.unit-spec.ts \
  test/frequently-bought-together.e2e-spec.ts \
  test/rest/email-recommendations.service.e2e-spec.ts
```

Commit message format: `RECO-678: fix require-object-params violations in style-copurchases and frequently-bought-together`

## Step 6: Push

```bash
git push -u origin RECO-678-fix-require-object-params-style-copurchases
```

## Step 7: Create PR

Use `gh pr create` targeting the base branch this branch was cut from. Check what branch this was cut from:
```bash
git log --oneline -5
git show-branch | grep '*' | grep -v "$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)" | head -1
```

The base branch should be `main` (check recent commits to confirm).

Create PR:
```bash
gh pr create --title "RECO-678: fix require-object-params violations in style-copurchases and frequently-bought-together" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Fix `custom-rules/require-object-params` ESLint violations in `style-copurchases` and `frequently-bought-together` modules
- Update all callers in `email-recommendations`, CLI commands, and test files
- All functions now use single object destructuring parameter pattern

## Files Changed
- `src/modules/db/style-copurchases/models/style-copurchase.model.ts`
- `src/modules/db/style-copurchases/style-copurchases.service.ts`
- `src/modules/cli/commands/populate-style-copurchases.command.ts`
- `src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.service.ts`
- `src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.controller.ts`
- `src/modules/rest/email-recommendations/email-recommendations.service.ts`
- `src/unit-tests/populate-style-copurchases.command.unit-spec.ts`
- `test/frequently-bought-together.e2e-spec.ts`
- `test/rest/email-recommendations.service.e2e-spec.ts`

## Test plan
- [ ] `npm run test:unit` passes
- [ ] `npm run test:e2e` passes
- [ ] `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` passes on all changed files

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
```

Return the PR URL when done.

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- Working directory: `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service`
- Current branch: `RECO-678-fix-require-object-params-style-copurchases`
- Do NOT branch from main - stay on current branch
- Fix ALL ESLint warnings before committing
- The `validate` function from `class-validator` should NOT be wrapped in object params - only custom functions need that

```bash
- Fix `custom-rules/require-object-params` ESLint violations in `style-copurchases` and `frequently-bought-together` modules
- Update all callers in `email-recommendations`, CLI commands, and test files
- All functions now use single object destructuring parameter pattern

## Files Changed
- `src/modules/db/style-copurchases/models/style-copurchase.model.ts`
- `src/modules/db/style-copurchases/style-copurchases.service.ts`
- `src/modules/cli/commands/populate-style-copurchases.command.ts`
- `src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.service.ts`
- `src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.controller.ts`
- `src/modules/rest/email-recommendations/email-recommendations.service.ts`
- `src/unit-tests/populate-style-copurchases.command.unit-spec.ts`
- `test/frequently-bought-together.e2e-spec.ts`
- `test/rest/email-recommendations.service.e2e-spec.ts`

## Test plan
- [ ] `npm run test:unit` passes
- [ ] `npm run test:e2e` passes
- [ ] `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` passes on all changed files

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
```

Return the PR URL when done.
